messages.body:
$AVGY.TSX  🐂 Bullish news about Broadcom 🔴 
"OPENAI AND BROADCOM  
$AVGO 
 JUST UNVEILED OPENAI'S FIRST CUSTOM AI CHIP 
 
It is called Jalapeño. It is an inference accelerator built from scratch specifically for large language models. 
 
Designed from scratch to manufacturing tape-out in nine months. OpenAI believes this is the fastest ASIC development cycle ever achieved in high-performance advanced semiconductors. 
 
Broadcom CEO Hock Tan: 
 
"This is just the beginning of a multi-generation roadmap. By co-developing our industry-leading silicon directly with OpenAI, we are enabling the deployment of gigawatt scale data centers with Microsoft and other partners beginning in 2026." 
 
Deployment starts by end of 2026 across multiple data center partners at gigawatt scale." 
https://x.com/StockMKTNewz/status/2069768286955307050?s=20

messages.body:
$AVGO CEO says Jalapeno will scale out over 2027 & reach full bore production in 2028 
 
OpenAI & Broadcom unveiled "Jalapeño," a custom "Intelligence Processor" tailored specifically for LLM inference. Developed in just 9 months, the chip is set for initial deployment at gigawatt-scale data centers 
 
Built from scratch around OpenAI’s specific model req'ts, Jalapeño utilizes Broadcom & $CLS for implementation, rack integration, & networking. Engineering samples are already running active machine learning workloads—including OpenAI’s GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark—at production-target frequencies & power levels in the lab 
 
Early lab testing indicates the architecture delivers a substantial performance-per-watt advantage over current market alternatives by minimizing data movement & optimizing the balance b/n compute, memory, & networking 
 
OpenAI plans to deploy the chip at a gigawatt scale across data center partners like $MSFT  beginning in 2026. The platform will leverage Broadcom’s Tomahawk networking silicon for its large-scale deployment capabilities 
 
 With these massive infrastructure contracts—including customers like $META & Anthropic—Broadcom has set a floor of over $100B in anticipated AI chip reves for 2027

messages.body:
$AVGO just took another big step deeper into the AI infra stack with OpenAI. 
 
They just unveiled “Jalapeño”  OpenAI’s first custom AI chip built specifically for LLM inference. 
 
What stood out to me is not just the chip, but how fast it came together: 
~9 months from design to tape-out 
One of the fastest ASIC cycles in high-end semis (by their claim) 
Purpose-built for inference, not general compute 
 
Hock Tan basically framed this as the start of a multi-generation roadmap, not a one-off experiment. 
 
Why it matters: 
Co-designed silicon with OpenAI 
Targeting GW-scale AI data centers 
Rollout starting end of 2026 with Microsoft and other hyperscalers 
 
This feels less like “chip news” and more like AI infrastructure getting vertically integrated in real time. 
 
$AVGO is sitting right in the middle of that shift.

messages.body: $AVGO

$AVGO is quietly getting deeper into the AI infrastructure shift with OpenAI. 
 
Hock Tan said OpenAI already received early “Jalapeño” chip samples and is testing real AI workloads on them. 
 
What really caught my attention: 
~50% cost savings vs traditional GPUs in inference tests (early stage) 
 
If that holds up at scale, it’s a big deal  because inference cost is becoming the real bottleneck in AI, not training. 
 
Why it matters: 
Cheaper inference = faster AI scaling economics 
Stronger lock-in between hyperscalers and custom silicon 
More shift from generic GPUs to purpose-built chips 
 
Still early, but this is exactly how platform shifts usually start  small tests, then sudden scale. 
 
$AVGO is positioning right inside that transition.

messages.body:
$AVGO just pushed deeper into the AI infra race with OpenAI, unveiling “Jalapeño”  OpenAI’s first custom chip built specifically for LLM inference. 
 
This doesn’t feel like a typical product drop. It feels more like the start of a long-term infrastructure partnership around how AI actually gets deployed at scale. 
 
What stands out to me: 
Broadcom calling this a multi-generation roadmap, not a one-off project 
Targeting gigawatt-scale AI data centers starting around 2026 
$MSFT and other hyperscalers already tied into the ecosystem 
 
The real story here is inference. Training gets the headlines,  
but inference is where the long-term cost and scale pressure lives.  
That’s exactly where custom silicon starts to matter. 
 
If this plays out, $AVGO isn’t just selling chips anymore  it’s embedding itself into the core architecture of AI data centers.
